Tracking problems with U.S. sky spies

Mike Fabey September-October 2006
Investigative MethodsData & DatabasesPublic Safety

Reporter Mike Fabey used federal contract databases and technical research to investigate budget overruns and mismanagement in the Pentagon's classified satellite programs.

Tired truckers endanger Tenn. drivers

Nancy Amons September-October 2006
Investigative MethodsPublic SafetyData & Databases

An investigation by WSMV-Nashville analyzed five years of Tennessee truck accident reports to determine the prevalence of driver fatigue in fatal and injury crashes.

Poor conditions plague medical helicopters

Alan Levin July-August 2006
Public SafetyInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

A USA Today investigation using a custom database discovered that lax industry safeguards and poor visibility lead to a high rate of fatal air ambulance helicopter crashes.

Maps identify at-risk areas

John Kelly November/December 2005
Public SafetyDemographics & CensusInvestigative Methods

Florida Today used GIS to identify thousands of residents in hurricane-prone areas who were eligible for evacuation assistance but had not signed up for the program.

GIS, GPS after the storm

Matthew Waite November/December 2005
Investigative MethodsTechnologyPublic Safety

Matthew Waite explains how to use GPS and GIS tools to rapidly map hurricane destruction, providing better visual context and reporting for readers.

November/December 2005
Public SafetyInvestigative Methods

IRE provides comprehensive resources for journalists covering boating accidents, including databases, tipsheets, and archived articles accessible through their dedicated webpage.
